vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1509: fix(plan-finalize): auto-promote chain produces 'proposed' specs but no work agents spawn (RUN-11 systemic)

plan.title: fix(plan-finalize): auto-promote chain produces 'proposed' specs but no work agents spawn (RUN-11 systemic)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The pan plan finalize → complete-planning → start-agent chain shipped in commit a3b0a857f is producing specs at plan.status=proposed on main but no work agents auto-spawn. Observed for 10 issues across RUN-11 ticks 11-12 (PAN-863, 1175, 1248, 1256, 1257, 1316, 1450, 1488, 1491, 1496). Direct evidence shows two distinct failure modes: (1) atomicity bug — completePlanningArtifacts writes the proposed spec to disk BEFORE running createBeadsFromVBrief, then throws if beads count != plan items, leaving an orphan proposed spec (confirmed for PAN-1450 8/10, PAN-863 1/12, PAN-1496 0/9 beads vs plan items); (2) chain fragility — the CLI-driven 3-step chain has a 1.5s deferred killSession from complete-planning that races against the second POST (spawnWorkAgent), so when /api/agents takes >1.5s due to docker stack-health probes the planning session is killed mid-fetch and the spawn never reaches the server (suspected for the other 6/10 where beads ARE materialized correctly). Disk pressure (ENOSPC observed in dashboard.log) and dashboard restarts between the two POSTs make the chain irrecoverable without manual intervention. There is no server-side reconciliation safety net.
narratives.Proposal: Fix the chain at the root by (1) restoring atomicity in completePlanningArtifacts — materialize beads FIRST, write the proposed spec SECOND, so 'proposed on main' always implies matching beads on disk; (2) collapse the 3-step chain into a single server-side call — complete-planning route accepts autoSpawn=true and runs the work-agent spawn in the same Effect after spec+beads succeed, eliminating the CLI-to-CLI HTTP race entirely; (3) defer the planning-session kill until after the work-agent spawn completes (or explicitly fails) so plan-finalize CLI survives long enough to surface errors; (4) add a Deacon reconciliation patrol that scans .pan/specs/ each cycle for orphan proposed specs and re-attempts spawn — this is the safety net that catches dashboard restarts, transient docker failures, and any future regressions in the primary chain; (5) add a pan doctor check listing orphan proposed specs so operators see the state immediately (per AC4 in the issue); (6) add structured activity-log events at each step of the chain so future RUN-N debugging is tractable; (7) regression test exercising the full chain end-to-end (per AC3).

autoDecisions.summary: Architect the fix as server-side autoSpawn (one HTTP call from plan-finalize CLI) rather than CLI-side retries with backoff.
autoDecisions.rationale: The chain race between CLI fetch and server-side killSession is structural — adding retries papers over symptoms. A single server-side transaction eliminates the race class entirely. Aligns with the no-bandaids rule in CLAUDE.md.

autoDecisions.summary: Use beads-first / spec-second atomicity rather than a transactional rollback (write spec, then delete on failure).
autoDecisions.rationale: Simpler invariant to reason about and to verify in tests: if proposed-on-main, then matching beads exist. No partial-rollback edge cases. The cost is one more disk write delay before the spec lands, which is negligible compared to bead creation time.

autoDecisions.summary: Defer planning-session kill until AFTER autoSpawn rather than skipping the kill outright.
autoDecisions.rationale: The session must still be cleaned up — leaving it alive forever creates clutter and false 'active planning agent' signals in the dashboard. Deferring the kill until after spawn completes preserves the cleanup AND keeps plan-finalize CLI alive long enough to surface errors. autoSpawn=false (kanban Done button) keeps the existing immediate-kill behavior.

autoDecisions.summary: Keep the stack-health gate active for auto-spawn — do NOT add a host=true bypass through the chain.
autoDecisions.rationale: The stack-health gate exists to prevent half-configured agents from running against an unhealthy docker stack. Bypassing it would mask real docker problems. The reconciler retries on subsequent cycles once docker recovers, so the gate's rejection is non-terminal.

autoDecisions.summary: Add the Deacon reconciler as a safety net even after fixing the primary chain.
autoDecisions.rationale: Even a fully-atomic server-side chain can fail to spawn for legitimate transient reasons (docker unhealthy, provider quota, dashboard restart). The reconciler ensures the system self-heals without operator intervention — which is the entire point of --auto mode. This is defense in depth, not redundancy.

autoDecisions.summary: Increase plan-finalize CLI fetch timeout from 30s to 90s when autoSpawn=true.
autoDecisions.rationale: complete-planning route now does spec-write + beads-materialize + docker-health-probe + agent-spawn in one call. 30s is too tight under load. 90s gives headroom for bd doctor --fix retries and docker startup.

autoDecisions.summary: Doctor check classifies orphan proposed specs by reason (beads-mismatch, no-agent, beads-zero, disk-full hint, dashboard-down hint) rather than a generic 'orphan' bucket.
autoDecisions.rationale: Operators reading doctor output need actionable next steps. Classifying by reason maps directly to remediation (re-run planning, retry spawn, free disk, restart dashboard). Generic warnings get ignored.

items.title: fix(complete-planning): materialize beads before writing proposed spec

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts, completePlanningArtifacts currently writes the proposed spec to disk via writeSpec/writeSpecForIssue (lines ~122-129) BEFORE running createBeadsFromVBrief (lines ~131-138), and throws on bead count mismatch (lines ~142-147). Reverse the order: run createBeadsFromVBrief first; only on full success (beadsResult.success && created.length === planItemCount) write the proposed spec. On bead failure, throw without writing the spec. Keep the existing bd-doctor retry logic inside createBeadsFromVBrief intact. Update src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/complete-planning.test.ts to assert the new ordering: a deliberate bead-creation failure must leave the spec file untouched (or absent if it never existed) on disk.

subItems.title: Bead creation runs before spec write in completePlanningArtifacts
If createBeadsFromVBrief returns success=false OR created.length !== planItemCount, the proposed spec is NOT written/updated on disk
Existing successful path (beads count matches plan items) still writes the proposed spec at the same .pan/specs/ path as before
Test in __tests__/complete-planning.test.ts asserts the failure case leaves no proposed spec on disk
bd-doctor retry logic inside createBeadsFromVBrief is unchanged (no edits to src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts)
npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass

items.title: feat(complete-planning): autoSpawn=true spawns work agent server-side after spec+beads succeed

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts, extend the POST /api/issues/:id/complete-planning route to accept autoSpawn?: boolean in the request body (default false — preserves the kanban 'Done planning' button behavior). When autoSpawn=true AND spec+beads succeeded, invoke the same work-agent spawn logic that POST /api/agents uses today (factor out the existing logic in src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts lines ~2300-2900 into a shared helper if cleanest, OR call the existing handler via an internal in-process Effect invocation, OR call the /api/agents endpoint via localhost fetch — pick the cleanest given current architecture). The response shape gains optional fields: workAgentSpawned: boolean, workAgentSession?: string, workAgentError?: string, workAgentSkipReason?: string (e.g. 'stack-unhealthy', 'guardrails', 'paused'). All existing response fields (success, newState, gitPushed, beadsWarning, message) remain backward-compatible. When autoSpawn=true and the spawn is rejected by stack-health or guardrails, return 200 (NOT an error) with workAgentSpawned: false plus the reason — the reconciler will retry on later cycles.

subItems.title: Request body accepts optional autoSpawn boolean; default false; field omitted from existing callers does not change behavior
When autoSpawn=true and spec+beads succeed, work agent is spawned in the same Effect; response includes workAgentSpawned=true and workAgentSession
When autoSpawn=true and stack-health rejects the spawn, response returns 200 with workAgentSpawned=false and workAgentSkipReason='stack-unhealthy' (NOT a 4xx/5xx)
When autoSpawn=true and spec+beads fail (e.g. bead mismatch), the route returns 5xx as today; no spawn attempted
When autoSpawn=false (kanban Done button), behavior is byte-for-byte identical to current main branch (regression test covers this)
Spawn reuses the same evaluateAgentStartGate + spawnGuardrails + stack-health checks as POST /api/agents — does NOT bypass any gate
New unit test in __tests__/complete-planning.test.ts asserts autoSpawn=true happy path and stack-health-rejection path
npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass

items.title: fix(complete-planning): defer planning-session kill until after autoSpawn completes

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts, the complete-planning route schedules setTimeout(killSession, 1500) at lines ~1276-1284 unless skipKill=true. When autoSpawn=true, this 1.5s deferred kill races against the work-agent spawn (which can take >1.5s under load). Move the killSession invocation to AFTER the spawn step has resolved (success or fail). For autoSpawn=true, the kill fires immediately on the Effect's success branch (no setTimeout needed — the response has already been generated by the handler's return value). For autoSpawn=false, keep the existing 1.5s deferred kill behavior (CLI compatibility). skipKill=true still skips the kill in both cases.

subItems.title: When autoSpawn=true, planning-pan-<id> tmux session is killed AFTER the spawn step has resolved (success or fail), not on a 1.5s timer
When autoSpawn=false, the existing 1.5s deferred kill behavior is preserved
When skipKill=true, no kill happens (existing behavior preserved in both branches)
If the spawn throws/rejects, the planning session is STILL killed (no leaked tmux session)
Unit test in __tests__/complete-planning.test.ts asserts kill timing in both autoSpawn=true success and failure paths

items.title: fix(plan-finalize): use server-side autoSpawn; remove second POST to /api/agents

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts, replace the current 3-step chain (createBeadsFromVBrief locally → promotePlanning → spawnWorkAgent) with a 2-step chain: createBeadsFromVBrief locally (kept — this primes the workspace bd database for the work agent that runs in the same workspace) → POST /api/issues/:id/complete-planning with body { autoSpawn: true }. Remove the separate spawnWorkAgent function (lines 198-230) and its invocation (lines 107-111). The new HTTP response includes workAgentSpawned/workAgentSession/workAgentError/workAgentSkipReason fields — surface those in the CLI console output the same way the current code does (green ✓ on spawn success, yellow ⚠ on skip with the reason, dim hint on how to retry). Increase the fetch timeout from 30s to 90s for this call since it now does spec+beads+spawn server-side. Update src/cli/commands/__tests__/plan-finalize.test.ts to assert the new single-POST behavior and absence of any direct /api/agents call.

subItems.title: plan-finalize.ts no longer contains a spawnWorkAgent function or a fetch to /api/agents
promotePlanning POSTs body { autoSpawn: true } (or equivalent), and the fetch timeout is 90000ms
Console output surfaces workAgentSpawned / workAgentSkipReason / workAgentError with actionable hints
--no-promote still skips both promotion and spawn (preserved opt-out)
JSON output mode preserves the workAgentSpawned / workAgentMessage / workAgentError shape so any consumer code is unaffected
__tests__/plan-finalize.test.ts asserts only one POST is made (no fetch to /api/agents) and the body includes autoSpawn
npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass

items.title: feat(deacon): reconcile orphan proposed specs (auto-spawn safety net)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts (or a new src/lib/cloister/orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts wired into deacon's patrol loop), add a periodic reconciler that scans <projectRoot>/.pan/specs/ for vBRIEF files with plan.status='proposed'. For each, check (a) no active agent-pan-<id> tmux session exists on the panopticon socket, (b) no ~/.panopticon/agents/agent-pan-<id>/state.json with status='starting' or 'running' exists, (c) beads count for the issue label matches plan.items.length, (d) issue is NOT paused/troubled/closed. When all four hold, the spec is an orphan — invoke the same work-agent spawn path that POST /api/agents uses (factor out the helper from server-side-autospawn bead if cleanest). On rejection (stack-unhealthy, guardrails, provider-down), log + skip — the next cycle retries. Run no more often than every 5 minutes to avoid thrash. Add a Deacon config gate (e.g. cloister.orphanProposedReconciler.enabled, default true) so operators can disable if it misbehaves.

subItems.title: Reconciler patrol runs on the existing Deacon cycle at no more than 1 attempt per issue per 5 minutes
Reconciler scans <projectRoot>/.pan/specs/ for plan.status='proposed' and identifies orphans (no active session, no in-flight state.json, beads match plan items, not paused/troubled/closed)
Orphan spawn uses the SAME gate/guardrail/stack-health checks as direct POST /api/agents — no bypasses
Paused, troubled, or closed issues are skipped (respects existing gates)
Reconciler emits structured activity-log events: scan-start, orphan-detected, spawn-attempt, spawn-success, spawn-skipped (with reason)
Reconciler can be disabled via config (cloister.orphanProposedReconciler.enabled=false); default is enabled
Unit test exercises orphan-detection logic against a tmp .pan/specs/ fixture with mixed states (proposed-orphan, proposed-with-agent, proposed-paused, completed)
npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass

items.title: feat(pan-doctor): detect orphan proposed specs and surface remediation

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/doctor.ts (and/or src/lib/doctor/* if checks are factored out), add a new check 'orphan-proposed-specs': for each project in projects.yaml, scan <projectRoot>/.pan/specs/ for plan.status='proposed' that have no active agent-pan-<id> session AND no state.json showing in-flight/running. Classify each orphan by reason: beads-zero (beadCount=0 — likely planning failure or disk-full), beads-mismatch (beadCount != planItemCount — partial bead creation), no-agent-no-reason (matches plan items but no agent — likely chain failure, retryable). Print actionable hints per category (e.g. 'free disk and re-run planning' for beads-zero, 'pan start <id> --host to bypass stack-health temporarily' for no-agent). The check is informational (warns, does not fail the doctor exit code unless a --strict flag is added later).

subItems.title: pan doctor includes a new 'orphan-proposed-specs' check that scans .pan/specs/ across all configured projects
Each orphan is classified by reason: beads-zero, beads-mismatch, no-agent-no-reason; each category has its own actionable hint
Check is informational (warn-level) and does not change pan doctor's exit code in the default invocation
Output groups orphans by project so operators can target remediation per repo
Unit test exercises the classification logic with a tmp fixture containing one orphan of each type
Wrapper skill at skills/pan-doctor/SKILL.md mentions the new check (per the SKILLS-CONVENTION rule)
npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass (including scripts/lint-skills.sh)

items.title: feat(activity-log): structured events across plan-finalize → complete-planning → spawn chain

narrative.Action: Add emitActivityEntrySync calls (or equivalent structured-log primitives already used by these modules) at each phase boundary so future RUN-N failures are debuggable without diffing dashboard.log: (1) src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts emits 'auto-promote.phase=createBeads', 'auto-promote.phase=completePlanning', 'auto-promote.phase=done' with success/failure/reason; (2) src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts complete-planning route emits 'complete-planning.phase=specWrite', '.phase=beadsMaterialize', '.phase=autoSpawn' with the same shape; (3) src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts /api/agents handler emits 'start-agent.phase=stackHealthGate', '.phase=guardrails', '.phase=spawn' on rejection/success. All events include issueId + ISO timestamp + reason string. Source label should be 'plan-finalize' or 'complete-planning' or 'start-agent' so dashboard log filters work cleanly.

subItems.title: plan-finalize CLI emits a structured event at each phase boundary (createBeads start/end, completePlanning start/end, terminal)
complete-planning route emits structured events for specWrite, beadsMaterialize, autoSpawn (when applicable), terminal
POST /api/agents emits structured events at each rejection point (stack-health, guardrails) and success
All events carry issueId, ISO timestamp, reason, and a stable source label suitable for grep/filter
No logging change introduces synchronous file I/O in dashboard server code (must use emitActivityEntrySync or equivalent already-async-safe primitive)
npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass

items.title: test: end-to-end regression for plan-finalize → autoSpawn → running work agent

narrative.Action: Add a vitest integration test (likely in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/ or a new src/lib/auto-promote-chain.integration.test.ts) that simulates the full chain: (1) set up a tmp project with a synthetic issue ID and a workspace containing a minimal valid .pan/spec.vbrief.json + matching beads; (2) invoke plan-finalize programmatically (importing planFinalizeCommand) or via execAsync against a built CLI; (3) assert that AFTER the call returns: (a) the proposed spec exists at <projectRoot>/.pan/specs/, (b) bead count matches plan items, (c) workAgentSpawned=true in the response, (d) ~/.panopticon/agents/agent-<issueLower>/state.json exists with status in {starting, running} within 60s, (e) tmux session agent-<issueLower> exists on the panopticon socket. Add a NEGATIVE test for the atomicity invariant: a plan with intentionally broken beads (e.g. a malformed bd database) must result in NO proposed spec on disk. Use vi.useFakeTimers() per CLAUDE.md for any retry delays. Mark with @slow if real-wall-clock waits are required, and exclude from default run if so.

subItems.title: Happy-path test: plan-finalize end-to-end produces proposed spec + matching beads + running work agent within 60s
Atomicity test: deliberate bead failure leaves NO proposed spec on disk
Reconciler test: a manually-planted orphan proposed spec (no agent, beads match) is picked up by the reconciler and a work agent is spawned on the next cycle
Stack-health-rejection test: when stack-health gate rejects, the response carries workAgentSpawned=false + workAgentSkipReason='stack-unhealthy' (not a 4xx/5xx)
All tests use vi.useFakeTimers() for retry/delay logic; no real setTimeout in test code paths (per CLAUDE.md)
npm test passes from workspace root after bun install (no @slow exclusions in default run unless documented)
